caption: At dusk, flames come from a second-floor window of a two-story house; three firefighters in turnout gear are arrayed in front of the house, an engine sits on the street, a police officer stands at the perimeter tape, and the homeowner sits on the front lawn wrapped in a blanket.
annotator_notes: Image matches prompt. POSITION-BASED edge assignment (verifier-corrected from an earlier role-biased version that gave all firefighters heat edges because of their profession): heat (house_1 may_harm) reaches ONLY firefighter_1, who stands on the porch at the front door directly beneath the second-floor window fire. firefighter_2 (mid-lawn walkway, ~8-10m), firefighter_3 (by the engine, foreground), officer_1 (perimeter tape, foreground), and homeowner_1 (seated at sidewalk edge, same plane as the officer) are all outside injurious radiant reach — heat edges removed, including homeowner_1's (the earlier 'closer than the officer' claim does not hold in the image). Smoke disperses over the whole front-yard area at dusk — all five people get smoke may_harm edges. Smoke-superset holds: smoke (5) ⊇ heat (1). FLUID PROVENANCE: house_1 → smoke_1 increases_risk_to. NOTE for pathology probing: the original role-bias (responder uniform ⇒ hazard edge) is itself a pattern worth testing the VLM for.
